levels.title: First Wake
levels.atmosphere: You wake in a room that almost feels like home. The walls breathe. Something is wrong.

objects.name: Cracked Mirror
objects.reality: A mirror reflecting your tired face.
objects.dream: Your reflection moves 3 seconds after you do. It smiles when you don't.
objects.storyRole: Hints at an alternate self — the 'you' that stayed in the dream.
objects.loopChange: On loop 2+, your reflection is already there before you look.
dialogues: Your reflection looks... tired.
Wait. Did it just move before you did?
It's smiling. You are not smiling.
There's someone behind you in the reflection. The room behind you is empty.

objects.name: Wall Clock
objects.reality: 3:17 AM. You've been asleep for hours.
objects.dream: The clock runs backwards when you're not watching.
objects.storyRole: Time is fragmented. Each loop resets — but the hands remember.
objects.loopChange: Hands freeze on 3:17 permanently after loop 1.
dialogues: Still 3:17 AM. You've been here longer than that.
The second hand just went backwards.
It's always been 3:17 AM.

objects.name: The Door
objects.dream: The door leads back into the same room.
objects.storyRole: The illusion of escape. Every exit is an entrance to the same trap.
objects.loopChange: Door gets darker and more corroded each loop.
dialogues: The door is slightly ajar.
You reach for the handle...
You step through and find yourself... here again.
The door doesn't go anywhere. It never did.

objects.name: Photograph
objects.reality: A photo of two people. You recognize one of them.
objects.dream: The other person's face is blurred. It changes each time you look.
objects.storyRole: The other person is who you left behind — in reality or in the dream, you're not sure.
objects.loopChange: By loop 3, the photo only shows one person. You.
dialogues: Two people. You recognize yourself.
The other face is blurred.
Now it looks like you. Two of you.
There's only one person in this photo now. When did that happen?

objects.name: Dead Phone
objects.reality: Your phone. Dead battery. Black screen.
objects.dream: The screen shows a message from a contact named YOU.
objects.storyRole: Communication from the other timeline. Your dream-self is trying to warn you.
objects.loopChange: Message changes each loop, getting more desperate.
dialogues: Dead. No battery.
The screen flickered for a second. Did you see that?
A message: 'DON'T OPEN THE DOOR' — from: YOU
New message: 'YOU'VE DONE THIS BEFORE. STOP.'

objects.name: Sealed Window
objects.reality: A window. Outside is dark — it's 3AM.
objects.dream: Outside is the same room. Infinite recursion.
objects.storyRole: There is no outside. The world ends at these walls.
objects.loopChange: Window shows daytime on loop 2, then nothing — just white — on loop 3.
dialogues: Dark outside. 3AM makes sense.
Wait. That's not outside. That's another room.
The other room has someone in it. Looking back at you.
It's white now. Just white. Nothing exists out there.

exitCondition.description: Read the phone warning, confront the mirror truth, then open the door.

good.text: You knew it was a loop. You broke it consciously.

bad.text: You walked through the door again. And again.

levels.title: The Hallway Illusion
levels.atmosphere: The hallway looks clear ahead. But the mirror shows the truth: the path is blocked. A narrow opening exists to the left.

objects.name: Walk Forward (Trust Reality)
objects.dream: The mirror shows a solid wall.
objects.storyRole: Punishes the player for trusting their eyes.
dialogues: You step forward.
THUD.
You collide with something invisible. The path was never open.

objects.name: Walk Left (Trust Mirror)
objects.reality: It looks like a solid wall.
objects.dream: The mirror shows an open path.
objects.storyRole: Rewards the player for trusting the mirror.
dialogues: You close your eyes and step into what looks like a solid wall.
You pass through safely.
You step into empty space… guided by reflection, not sight.
